title: Node 24, TypeScript 7.0.2, TypeScript 6 API Compatibility, npm, and Repro… · Delivery-System Trace: Ci Clean Build Source Artifact Boundary
summary: A TypeScript build uses floating Node and npm versions, npm install mutates the lockfile, lifecycle scripts gain network authority, and local generated files hide a clean-run failure. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci clean build source artifact boundary increment in reproducible TypeScript build contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build from a clean checkout, exclude local residue, declare generated inputs, and compare packaged output with source and ignore rules.
Bound dependency lifecycle scripts, registry authentication, private package access, network egress, install auditing, and secret exposure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

steps.title: Predict Ci Clean Build Source Artifact Boundary · Theory Ci Clean Build Source Artifact Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript build floats Node and npm, mutates its lockfile, runs dependency lifecycle scripts, calls a programmatic API that TypeScript 7 does not provide, and passes only with warm local residue. Prove Node 24, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API, npm ci, clean output, and changed-environment behavior. Competency probe: Build from a clean checkout, exclude local residue, declare generated inputs, and compare packaged output with source and ignore rules.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example TypeScript build floats Node and npm, mutates its lockfile, runs dependency lifecycle scripts, calls a programmatic API that TypeScript 7 does not provide, and passes only with warm local residue. Prove Node 24, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API, npm ci, clean output, and changed-environment behavior. Competency probe: Build from a clean checkout, exclude local residue, declare generated inputs, and compare packaged output with source and ignore rules. A defensible solution applies Ci Clean Build Source Artifact Boundary, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reproducible TypeScript build contract.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-ci-node-typescript-reproducibility-2
  - competency: ci-worked-ci-node-typescript-reproducibility-2
    artifact: typescript-build.yaml
    decision: worked ci node typescript reproducibility 2
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      node: 24-lts
      npm: 11-or-later
      typescript-native: 7.0.2
      typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
      tsc: native-compiler
      api-tools: typescript6-alias
      setup-node: 7
      install: npm-ci-frozen-lock
      package-manager-cache: risk-reviewed
      workspace: clean-checkout
      build-output: content-digested
      changed-environment: cold-cache-and-locale
    verify: npm ci then run strict type test build and package checks from a clean supported Node 24 environment
    failure: Reject floating toolchains, npm install lock mutation, local residue, lifecycle-script trust, or one warm build as reproducibility.
    repair: Align tool versions, freeze installs, bound registry and scripts, build cleanly, digest output, and repeat changed environments.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
